message:
fix(demo_showcase): harden truncate_line_ansi_aware against incomplete escapes

Apply the same safety pattern from the lipgloss style.rs fix to the
demo_showcase's independent ANSI truncation function:

- Consume the char after ESC immediately and push it before branching,
  eliminating an unwrap on chars.next() for simple two-char escapes
- CSI sequence loop: replace unwrap() inside the peek-consume pattern
  with if-let, and compare char as u32 instead of u8 to correctly handle
  non-ASCII escape terminators
- OSC/string-type sequences: replace peek-based ST detection with
  prev_was_esc flag tracking, combining BEL and ST termination into a
  single condition and removing the inner unwrap

Regression tests added:
- truncate_line_ansi_aware_handles_trailing_escape: lone ESC at end
- truncate_line_ansi_aware_handles_incomplete_csi_sequence: ESC[ with
  no final byte
- truncate_line_ansi_aware_handles_unterminated_string_escape: ESC]
  without BEL or ST terminator

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
